rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation-carboxylation and -deuteration of alkenes using difluoromethyltriphenylphosphonium bromide as a CF2H radical precursor with CO2 or D2O as electrophile
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of [Ph3PCF2H]+Br- generates a CF2H radical; the benzylic radical adduct is further cathodically reduced to a benzyl anion which traps CO2 or D2O; Al anode is sacrificially oxidized to Al3+

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical from cathodic reduction of phosphonium salt
Benzylic radical A from CF2H addition to alkene
Benzyl anion B from further cathodic reduction of A

electron_flow: Cathode reduces [Ph3PCF2H]+ to give CF2H radical (extruding PPh3); the CF2H radical adds to the alkene to form a benzylic radical, which is reduced again at the cathode to a benzyl anion that attacks CO2 (then esterified with TMSCHN2) or D2O. Al anode is oxidized sacrificially.

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene radical clock with D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened HF2C-CH=CH-CH2-CH(Ph)-D product detected by HRMS, supporting CF2H radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene radical clock with CO2
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product with CF2H and COOH groups detected by HRMS, supporting radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene with D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product formed in 63% yield with 86% D incorporation, consistent with benzyl anion intermediate trapped by D2O

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of phosphonium salt and DPE
probe_reagents.observation: [Ph3PCF2H]+Br- is more easily reduced (-1.640 V) than 1,1-diphenylethylene (-2.562 V); mixture shows new wave suggesting radical anion of alkene possible at more negative potentials

notes: Authors propose a minor pathway where the alkene is reduced to its radical anion C, which couples with CF2H radical to give anion B (radical-radical coupling); the major pathway is sequential CF2H radical addition followed by second cathodic reduction to the benzyl anion. X-ray structure (CCDC 2281142) of carboxylation product A-1 confirms regiochemistry. Monosubstituted alkenes give low yields due to instability of intermediate relative to diphenyl-stabilized anions.
